title: Introduction and Research Context
objective: Provide background on pedestrian safety at signalized intersections and the emergence of autonomous vehicle-pedestrian interaction research, establishing the scope and relevance of reviewed studies.

title: Methodological Approaches in Pedestrian Behavior Research
objective: Compare and contrast the experimental and observational methods used across reviewed papers, including virtual reality experiments, surveys, and discrete choice modeling techniques.

title: Autonomous Vehicle-Pedestrian Interaction at Crosswalks
objective: Synthesize findings from the virtual reality experiment on external interface features for fully autonomous vehicles, including pedestrian receptivity, feature preferences, and demographic differences.

title: Pedestrian Red-Light Running Behavior
objective: Synthesize evidence on factors influencing pedestrian red-light running violations, including personal characteristics, environmental factors, and social influences from Chinese-context studies.

title: Comparative Analysis and Evidence Synthesis
objective: Compare findings across studies on pedestrian crossing behavior, identify methodological strengths and limitations, and evaluate the generalizability of conclusions across different traffic contexts.

title: Research Limitations and Future Directions
objective: Discuss methodological limitations across the reviewed literature, including sample size constraints, geographic scope, and measurement approaches, and propose directions for future pedestrian behavior research.
